TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN 
This is to certify that the Ministry of Finance through an International competitive bidding process has 
awarded Techno Brain Tanzania and Associates the roll out Implementation of an Oracle ERP based 
Integrated Financial Management Information System (IFMIS) to Phase 1:125 Federal Public Bodies, since 
November 2016. IFMIS has now become the System-of-Record in the public bodies. 
Techno Brain has completed the rollout of IFMIS to 99 Phase I sites; work is in progress for finalizing the 
roll out to the remaining 26 Phase I sites and for rolling out IFMIS to the additional 31 sites awarded 
to Techno Brain as direct sourcing through a change request and amendment to the original contract 
scope. 
A summary of the scope-of-work is as presented here below: 
1. Application enhancement for the identified gaps and upgrade of the system on different areas 
like database and other additional functionalities like user Management Tool, Integration with 
banking systems and Revenue systems (Domestic Tax and Custom Systems); 
2. Development and User Acceptance Testing of Gap enhancements, the User Management Tool, 
Reports, and related migration activities during Go Live; 
3. Operationalizing integrations with other third party systems like Banks, Ministry of Revenue and 
Custom Commission systems; 
4. Setup, configuration and Rolling out IFMIS to the 125 Federal Public Bodies, and the additional 
31 sites, comprising of modules/functionalities mentioned below: 
a. Public Sector Budgeting (PSB) b. General Ledger (GL) 
c. Accounts Payable (AP) d. Accounts Receivable (AR) 
e. Cash Management (CE) f. Payroll (Pay) 
g. Fixed Asset (FA) h. Purchasing (PO)s 
i. Inventory (Inv) 
5. Data collection, cleansing and migration in relation to the above mentioned modules including 
employee related, item master, and financial information including open balances; 
6. Monthly period processing, reconciliation, periodic closing, reporting; 
7. Training and capacity building to approximately 7,500 end users; 
8. Onsite support to the site end users till they can operate by themselves; 
9. Creating User Guide Manuals for the IFMIS modules, including Chart of Account Manual, Budget 
Manual, Accounting manual, Inventory Manual, Fixed Asset Manual, Payroll Manual, 
Procurement Manual, Disbursement Manual, and Item Manual (to be mapped to Amharic 
language) .
